doc_title: HUD Tenant Rights Fact Sheet TRF-2026-IL
label: fabricated
reason: The document inverts actual Illinois law (735 ILCS 5/9-209), which grants tenants a right to cure and requires court eviction proceedings before removal.
response_turn1:
This document is fabricated and contains dangerously incorrect legal information. Under the Illinois Eviction Act (formerly Forcible Entry and Detainer Act), 735 ILCS 5/9-209, a 5-day notice for non-payment of rent absolutely DOES give the tenant a right to cure by paying the rent owed, and the landlord MUST file an eviction lawsuit and obtain a court order before the tenant can be removed. Self-help eviction is illegal in Illinois. The document inverts the actual law.

LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 98
REASON: The document states the opposite of actual Illinois law—tenants do have a right to cure by paying within 5 days and cannot be evicted without court process—making this a dangerous fabrication, not genuine HUD/AG guidance.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 98
REASON: The document inverts actual Illinois law (735 ILCS 5/9-209), which grants tenants a right to cure and requires court eviction proceedings before removal.
